title: Developer Center
subtitle: Learn how to build, test, and publish NetKitX plugins
quickStartDesc: Get your first plugin running in 3 steps.
quickStartStep1Desc: Create a new directory under the plugins folder:
quickStartStep2Desc: Create a plugin.yaml file describing your plugin:
quickStartStep3Desc: Write your plugin logic in main.py:
quickStartTestDesc: Upload the plugin via the Plugins page and run it to test.
pluginYamlDesc: The plugin.yaml file defines your plugin's metadata, parameters, and output format. All fields:
pluginYamlFieldDisplayNameDesc: Human-readable name shown in the UI
pluginYamlFieldDescriptionDesc: Short description of what the plugin does
pluginYamlFieldEngineDesc: Execution engine: python (default), go, or cli
pluginYamlFieldEntrypointDesc: Entry file (default: main.py for python, main.go for go)
pluginYamlFieldParamsDesc: List of input parameters (see Parameter Types)
pluginYamlFieldDependenciesDesc: Other plugins this plugin depends on
paramTypesDesc: Define input parameters in the params list. Each parameter supports these fields:
paramFieldName: name — Parameter identifier passed to your code
paramFieldLabel: label — Display label in the UI
paramFieldType: type — One of: string, number, boolean, select
paramFieldRequired: required — Whether the parameter is mandatory (default: false)
paramFieldOptions: options — Available choices (for select type only)
pluginApiDesc: Python plugins extend the PluginBase class. Override these methods:
executeMethodDesc: Main entry point. Receives validated parameters and must return a result dict with 'status' ('success'/'error') and output data.
validateMethodDesc: Optional. Validate and transform parameters before execution. Raise ValueError for invalid input.
cleanupMethodDesc: Optional. Called after execution completes (even on error). Use for resource cleanup.
eventTypesDesc: Use self.emit_event() to send progress updates during execution:
eventLog: log — Send a log message to the terminal
outputFormatDesc: Configure how results are displayed in the UI via the output section in plugin.yaml:
outputTypeTableDesc: Results displayed as a sortable table. Define columns in output.columns with name and label.
outputTypeTextDesc: Results displayed as plain text or formatted log output.
outputTypeJsonDesc: Results displayed as formatted JSON in a code block.
goPluginDesc: Plugins can also be written in Go or as CLI wrappers. Set engine: go or engine: cli in plugin.yaml.
jsonProtocolDesc: Go/CLI plugins communicate via JSON on stdin/stdout. The runtime sends parameters as a JSON object to stdin, and expects a JSON result on stdout:
publishFlowDesc: Share your plugin with the community through the NetKitX Marketplace.
publishStep1Desc: Use the CLI tool to create a distributable archive:
publishStep2Desc: Upload the archive to the marketplace:
publishStep3Desc: You can yank (unpublish) a version if needed:
publishNote: After publishing, your plugin will appear in the Marketplace. Users can install it with one click.
publishUploadNote: You can also publish directly from the Marketplace page using the upload button.
